prevalence.trend: stable; recurrent cases linked to diabetes and antibiotic use

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; high discomfort and recurrence burden

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical symptoms with microscopy (KOH/wet mount) when available; culture for recurrent or nonresponsive cases

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; recurrent cases require longer regimens

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often clinical; microscopy and culture mostly in urban centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Topical azoles or oral fluconazole; extended regimens for recurrent disease; address diabetes control
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Self-medication, misdiagnosis with BV, and limited lab confirmation
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Low-cost azoles widely available in pharmacies and clinics
